name: El Caroche — Circular desde Bicorp por la Solana de la Muela de Bicorp
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Bicorp are set within the diverse landscapes of La Canal de Navarrés in Valencia, characterized by the mountainous Caroig Massif and the prominent Pico Caroche. The region features varied terrains, including smooth paths, rocky sections, and rooty trails, with significant elevation changes. River systems like the Fraile River carve through gorges, creating a dynamic environment for mountain biking. This area offers a mix of challenging routes and scenic natural features. Best mountain bike trails around Bicorp The most popular mountain bike trail is El Caroche — Circular desde Bicorp por la Solana de la Muela de Bicorp, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 59 minutes to complete, known for its demanding sections and advanced skill requirements. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is The Muela de Millares and the Rincón del Camaro - Circular from Quesa, a difficult 38.4 miles (61.8 km) path. This route features varied terrain and significant elevation gain, requiring very good fitness. Local mountain bikers also love the Bicorp – Rincón del Camaro loop from Bicorp, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through the mountainous landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 10 minutes. Mountain biking around Bicorp is defined by the Caroig Massif, the Fraile River, and diverse terrains ranging from smooth to rocky and rooty sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy trails to very demanding rides. The routes in Bicorp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 240 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bicorp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Bicorp, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Bicorp offers nearly 50 mountain bike routes. The trails cater to various skill levels, with 4 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 24 difficult routes, ensuring options for beginners to advanced riders.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Bicorp?
A: Yes, Bicorp has 4 easy mountain bike routes that are suitable for families and those looking for a less strenuous ride. These trails typically feature smoother paths and less elevation gain, making them accessible for various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Bicorp?
A: The terrain around Bicorp is diverse, ranging from smooth paths to rocky and rooty sections. You'll encounter varying elevation gains, with some routes offering fast descents and others presenting steep, loose turns and switchbacks, especially in the mountainous Caroig Massif.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass by waterfalls or natural pools in Bicorp?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural water features. You can find routes that pass by the Fraile River, known for captivating pools and waterfalls like Charco del Paso, La Tosca, and El Purgatorio. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; also has a recreational area and allows swimming.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints accessible by mountain bike in Bicorp?
A: Many trails offer access to impressive natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e909564069/el-caroche-circular-desde-bicorp-por-la-solana-de-la-muela-de-bicorp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Caroche — Circular desde Bicorp por la Solana de la Muela de Bicorp&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, explores the area around Pico Caroche, a prominent feature of the Caroig Massif. You might also encounter viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4010315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Cinto de la Cabra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Bicorp?
A: Yes, Bicorp offers several excellent circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bicorp – Rincón del Camaro loop from Bicorp&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the mountainous landscape. Another challenging circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908615157/the-muela-de-millares-and-the-rincon-del-camaro-circular-from-quesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Muela de Millares and the Rincón del Camaro - Circular from Quesa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails in Bicorp that are suitable for advanced riders?
A: Bicorp is an excellent destination for advanced riders, with 24 difficult routes. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e909564069/el-caroche-circular-desde-bicorp-por-la-solana-de-la-muela-de-bicorp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Caroche — Circular desde Bicorp por la Solana de la Muela de Bicorp&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e908615157/the-muela-de-millares-and-the-rincon-del-camaro-circular-from-quesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Muela de Millares and the Rincón del Camaro - Circular from Quesa&lt;/a&gt; are known for their demanding sections, significant elevation gains, and technical challenges, sometimes requiring advanced skills or even pushing your bike.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Bicorp?
A: The mountain bike trails in Bicorp are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 16 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging nature of many routes, and the beautiful natural scenery of the Caroig Massif.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites I can explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Bicorp is rich in history. The Moreno Gorge, for example, is home to UNESCO World Heritage rock art sites, including the famous La Araña Cave with its unique honey-foraging depiction. You can also find historical monuments like the 16th-century Castillo Palacio del Señor and the 17th-century Fuerte de Las Pedrizas.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Bicorp?
A: Yes, for many popular areas, parking is available. For instance, the Benefetal recreational area, a significant natural attraction, offers parking facilities, making it a convenient starting point for exploring trails along the Fraile River.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Bicorp?
A: Bicorp's climate generally allows for mountain biking through much of the year. However, spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Summers can be hot, while winters are typically mild, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the mountain bike trails in Bicorp?
A: While specific trailside cafes might be limited, the town of Bicorp itself offers options for dining and refreshments. The Benefetal recreational area also includes a restaurant, providing a convenient stop for riders exploring that part of the region.
tips: Section of the route for mountain bike #9 (blue-intermediate category) that runs along the Carril de La Solana. It is a road with very pleasant views and a lot of fun to walk on a good surface, it goes up and down and curves everywhere that make it very entertaining.

With its peak at 1,126 meters above sea level, the El Caroche massif offers incredible views in all directions. The stretch of ascent from the pass to the top has steep slopes with a good surface. You can reach the pass by different routes. The views reward all the physical effort of reaching the top.
Very fun trail for mtb that runs next to the Hongares ravine and through a very beautiful grove. It allows to reach the Cueva de Las Arañas and the Chorrador de la Rebolla.

Archaeological site with views of the Chorrador de La Rebolla, the Hongares ravine. From the viewpoint to the chorrador you can take the path on foot to reach the caves that remain closed except by appointment to enter.
Beautiful pool of water with very striking colors and in a very quiet place. Ideal for a swim in the summer. It can be accessed by path both by bike and on foot.

The viewpoint gives you beautiful views of the town of Bicorp. This town was an Islamic farmhouse, which provided water to the orchards. At present, it is a very picturesque town with an infinite number of mountain and hiking routes, which are worth visiting.

waypoints.name: La Araña Cave and La Rebolla Waterfall
